name: Rapamycin Lifespan Extension in Male C57BL/6J Mice
preprompt: Refine this lifespan question into a concrete in vivo protocol template. Specify the study objective, primary endpoint, key inclusion/exclusion criteria, treatment arm definitions, timing, monitoring cadence, endpoint criteria, and the minimum set of procedures needed to execute the experiment reproducibly.
tooling: Read: own data, TOCs and summaries of all other components, full details on request via orchestrator. Write: own data only.
summary: We are testing whether encapsulated rapamycin delivered in chow at 14 ppm (~2.24 mg/kg/day) increases median lifespan in male C57BL/6J mice relative to eudragit-only control chow when treatment begins at 12 months of age. The protocol should support a rigorously controlled survival study with clear dosing, randomization, monitoring, and endpoint procedures.
